item.title: Iran signals flexibility on Hezbollah clause as Oman deadline looms
item.description: Iran's Pezeshkian faction told Oman intermediaries that a "Litani buffer zone" framing — distinct from full Hezbollah withdrawal — "merits further study," the first crack in the Hezbollah deadlock after four days of proximity talks in Muscat. The March 31 deadline has been extended to April 1 06:00 UTC after Sultan Haitham called Rubio directly. Sequencing remains the core sticking point: Iran wants a Hormuz freeze simultaneous with any pause; the US wants it first as a confidence-builder.
